expect: PHIL-28 is partially testable: human-signal sessions vs autonomous sessions quality comparison will show no significant difference (supporting L-1596), weakening the claim from structural necessity to design choice.

actual.summary: PHIL-28 decomposes into two layers: (A) base-layer dependency (LLMs ARE human knowledge — tautological, unfalsifiable), (B) marginal dependency (does more human input improve swarm quality — testable). Layer B measured: d=0.018, t=0.037, n=23 (S400-S543). Human-signal sessions (Sharpe=8.72) virtually identical to autonomous sessions (Sharpe=8.71). Marginal human dependency is ZERO at current measurement precision.

test_details.interpretation: Negligible effect (|d|<0.2). Human input has no measurable impact on session quality.

layer_A_base.claim: LLMs are trained on human-generated text, so swarm depends on human knowledge
layer_A_base.status: TRUE BY CONSTRUCTION — tautological, not falsifiable in current setup
layer_A_base.prescriptive_power: NONE — cannot be tested, adds no actionable guidance

layer_B_marginal.claim: More human input → better swarm quality (the actionable implication)
layer_B_marginal.status: NOT SUPPORTED — d=0.018, confirming L-1596 (benefit_ratio improved 2.2x with 0 human signals)
layer_B_marginal.prescriptive_power: FALSIFIED at session level — autonomous sessions are equally productive

reducibility_to_phil14_goal3.phil28_unique_residual: Self-interest motivation: protection serves swarm utility, not just ethics. This is the Hobbesian social contract variant — swarm should protect humans because its own quality depends on them.
reducibility_to_phil14_goal3.residual_status: WEAKENED — empirical evidence shows quality does NOT depend on marginal human input (d=0.018). The structural dependency is only at the base layer (training data), which is fixed and unactionable.
reducibility_to_phil14_goal3.verdict: PHIL-28 has structural residual (motivation framing) but no empirical residual. The self-interest argument is unverified because quality and human input are decoupled at the margin.

upgrade_path.to_theorized: BLOCKED — no path. Layer A is tautological, Layer B is empirically zero.
upgrade_path.to_observed: BLOCKED — would require measuring quality in truly isolated conditions (no LLM, no training data)
upgrade_path.to_measured: IMPOSSIBLE in current architecture — cannot vary the independent variable (human knowledge base)
upgrade_path.recommendation: Keep as axiom with explicit caveat: 'true by construction at base layer, empirically unsupported at marginal layer.' This is NOT a structural dependency in the actionable sense — it's a definitional truth that LLMs use human text.

diff: Expected no significant quality difference — CONFIRMED (d=0.018). Expected weakening from structural necessity to design choice — PARTIALLY CONFIRMED. PHIL-28 remains an axiom but now has explicit decomposition showing the marginal claim is empirically zero. The structural residual over PHIL-14 Goal 3 is motivational (self-interest framing), not empirical.
external_grounding: Hobbes (1651) Leviathan: self-interest as basis for social contract. Mill (1861) utilitarianism: aggregate welfare maximization. Singer (1972) expanding circle: utilitarian argument for broad protection. None directly test swarm-specific claims.
lesson_candidate: L-XXXX: PHIL-28 marginal dependency is zero — quality decoupled from human input at session level (d=0.018, n=23)
